seo.title: Building RAG Pipeline with Semantic Kernel + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build rag pipeline with Semantic Kernel + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building RAG pipelines with Semantic Kernel enables powerful document retrieval, context assembly, and LLM synthesis for enterprise applications. However, plugin chaining, planners, and memory components introduce risks like cascading errors, invalid plans, and hallucinated facts that can undermine reliability. Vex provides essential guardrails through kernel filters to verify source faithfulness, context relevance, and citation accuracy in high-risk scenarios.

architectureOverview.heading: RAG Pipeline Architecture with Semantic Kernel
architectureOverview.description:
A typical Semantic Kernel RAG pipeline starts with a vector store (e.g., Pinecone or Chroma) for semantic search on user queries. Retrieved documents are assembled into context by a retrieval plugin, then passed to a planner that chains synthesis plugins for response generation with source attribution. Memory stores conversation history for contextual recall, while function calling invokes external tools if needed.

The kernel orchestrates this via plugins (native or OpenAI functions), planners (e.g., Handlebars or Sequential), and memory components. Failures often occur in cross-plugin data flow or when planners generate invalid invocation sequences. Vex integrates as a post-function filter to validate outputs against verification needs like hallucination detection and citation accuracy.

This architecture scales for enterprise use but requires runtime checks to prevent contamination between retrieval, synthesis, and memory plugins.
architectureOverview.diagram:
User Query
    |
    v
[Vector Search] --> Retrieved Chunks
    |
    v
Kernel Planner --> [Context Assembly Plugin]
    |
    v
[LLM Synthesis + Attribution] --> Verified Response
    ^
    | Vex Filter (Source Faithfulness, Hallucination Check)
Memory Store <--> Conversation History

risks.name: Plugin chaining producing cascading errors
risks.description: Errors in retrieval plugin propagate to synthesis, causing irrelevant or failed responses.
risks.mitigation: Vex function result filters validate each plugin output before chaining.

risks.name: Planner generating invalid execution plans
risks.description: Handlebars planner creates non-existent function calls, halting execution.
risks.mitigation: Pre-validate plan steps with Vex schema checks on function invocations.

risks.name: Memory recall hallucinating past interactions
risks.description: Memory plugin fabricates conversation history, poisoning context.
risks.mitigation: Vex content filters detect hallucinated facts against stored memory.

risks.name: Cross-plugin data contamination
risks.description: Retrieval chunks mix with unrelated memory data, creating false attributions.
risks.mitigation: Vex citation verification ensures source-context isolation.

implementation.title: Basic RAG Kernel Setup
implementation.description: Initialize Semantic Kernel with vector search and synthesis plugins.
implementation.code:
from semantic_kernel import Kernel
from semantic_kernel.connectors.ai.open_ai import OpenAIChatCompletion

kernel = Kernel()
kernel.add_chat_service("gpt", OpenAIChatCompletion("gpt-4", "your-api-key"))

# Add retrieval plugin
kernel.import_plugin_from_object(RetrievalPlugin(vector_store), "retrieval")

# RAG function
rag_function = kernel.create_function_from_prompty("rag_prompt.prompty")
result = await kernel.invoke("rag_function", query="What is Semantic Kernel?")

vexIntegration.title: Python: Vex Kernel Function Filter
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex as post-invocation filter for source faithfulness and hallucination checks.
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexClient
from semantic_kernel import KernelFunction

vex = VexClient(api_key="your-vex-key")

def vex_rag_filter(result: KernelFunctionResult) -> bool:
    verdict = vex.verify({
        'policy': 'rag-high-risk',
        'input': result.variables['query'],
        'output': result.value,
        'verifications': ['source_faithfulness', 'hallucination_detection', 'context_relevance', 'citation_accuracy'],
        'sources': result.variables.get('sources', [])
    })
    return verdict.passed

# Register filter
kernel.register_function_result_filter("rag*", vex_rag_filter)

result = await kernel.invoke("rag_function", query="test")

vexIntegration.title: TypeScript: Vex Result Interceptor
vexIntegration.description: Intercept planner function results with Vex for citation validation.

vexIntegration.title: Python: Memory Recall Verification
vexIntegration.description: Filter memory plugin outputs to prevent hallucinated conversation history.
vexIntegration.code:
async def vex_memory_filter(result: str) -> bool:
    verdict = vex.verify({
        'policy': 'memory-safety',
        'input': {'type': 'memory_recall'},
        'output': result,
        'verifications': ['hallucination_detection', 'context_relevance'],
        'session_id': conversation.session_id
    })
    return verdict.passed

# Apply to memory plugin
memory_plugin.register_result_filter(vex_memory_filter)

history = await memory_plugin.get_conversation_history(user_id)
